info.name: Cloudflare DNS Auto-Report — API Explorer
info.description:
Explore and test the Cloudflare APIs used by the DNS Auto-Report system.

## Setup
1. Create a Postman Environment with:
   - `CF_API_TOKEN`   — your Cloudflare API token (Zone.DNS:Read, Zone.Zone:Read, Zone.Analytics:Read)
   - `CF_ZONE_ID`     — target zone ID (found in the Cloudflare dashboard Overview page)
   - `CF_ACCOUNT_ID`  — your Cloudflare account ID
   - `START_DATE`     — report start date, e.g. 2026-03-01
   - `END_DATE`       — report end date,   e.g. 2026-03-25

2. Run the requests in order, or use the Collection Runner to run all at once.

## Resources
- GraphQL API Explorer: https://graphql.cloudflare.com/explorer
- DNS Analytics docs:   https://developers.cloudflare.com/dns/additional-options/analytics/

request.description: Fetch zone metadata: name, status, nameservers, plan, etc.
List all zones in your account. Use zone IDs from this response in accounts.json.
Fetch all DNS records for the zone. Used in the DNS Records Inventory section of the report.
Fetch DNSSEC status and DS record for the zone.
Time-series DNS query count per day. Uses the correct `count` field (not sum.queryCount).
Cache hit vs miss breakdown. responseCached=true means served from cache.
Top 15 most-queried domain names in the period.
Breakdown of queries by record type (A, AAAA, MX, CNAME, TXT, etc.)
DNS response code distribution: NOERROR, NXDOMAIN, SERVFAIL, REFUSED, etc.
The full combined GraphQL query used by the report system — all five datasets in one round-trip using aliased fields. Uses `count` (correct field) instead of the invalid `sum.queryCount`.
Account-level query: find NXDOMAIN responses across ALL zones in your account. Useful for investigating DNS errors.
Verify that your API token is valid and check its status.
List all API tokens for your account (omits the actual token values).
